denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This notice concerns the request for coverage of a carfilzomib-based triplet (carfilzomib, daratumumab, and dexamethasone) administered with twice-weekly carfilzomib infusions for relapsed multiple myeloma. Cigna has completed a medical necessity review and has determined that the requested administration frequency does not meet coverage criteria.

Under the Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and the applicable MCG Care Guidelines, the requested twice-weekly carfilzomib infusion cadence exceeds the frequency we consider medically necessary for this therapy. Our reviewer concluded that a less frequent administration schedule would be expected to achieve the intended clinical benefit, and that the documentation does not establish why the more frequent cadence is required in this case. On that basis, coverage at the requested frequency is denied.

This determination reflects the visit-frequency standards described in the MCG Care Guidelines applied to your plan. The decision pertains to the cadence of administration; it is not a determination that the underlying agents are themselves excluded from coverage.

The request was received at 9:32 AM on May 19, 2026, and the determination was issued at 9:36 AM the same day, based on the information available at that time.

You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria applied to this review at no charge. You have the right to appeal this determination within 180 days of the date of this notice by submitting a written request together with any supporting clinical documentation. A peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director may be arranged on request.

Sincerely,
Cigna Coverage Review Department
clinical_context: The patient is a 64-year-old woman with relapsed multiple myeloma (C90.00). She was initially treated with a lenalidomide-bortezomib-dexamethasone induction followed by lenalidomide maintenance, on which she progressed during the first quarter of 2026 with a serum M-spike rising from 0.4 to 1.9 g/dL over four months and a reciprocal fall in uninvolved immunoglobulins, confirming biochemical relapse. Her treating hematologist selected a carfilzomib-daratumumab-dexamethasone triplet, for which twice-weekly carfilzomib (administered on days 1, 2, 8, 9, 15, and 16 of each cycle) is the FDA-labeled and NCCN-recognized administration schedule; the dosing density is intrinsic to the regimen rather than an elective increase in visits. Renal function is preserved with a stable creatinine of 0.9 mg/dL, and an echocardiogram showed a normal ejection fraction of 60 percent, supporting tolerability of the proteasome inhibitor schedule. The oncologist documented that a reduced infusion frequency is not an established equivalent for this combination and would deliver subtherapeutic exposure for an aggressive relapse.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Denial misapplies generic MCG visit-frequency standard to FDA-labeled twice-weekly carfilzomib; easy appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Misapplies a generic MCG visit-frequency standard to the FDA-labeled, NCCN-recognized twice-weekly carfilzomib schedule, where dosing density is intrinsic to the regimen, not an elective visit increase
Circular/conclusory clinical reasoning asserting a less frequent schedule would achieve the intended benefit without citing any validated equivalent, contradicted by the oncologist's documentation
Four-minute determination (received 9:32 AM, issued 9:36 AM) suggests inadequate individualized clinical review
strong_defenses: Letter is procedurally sound: discloses 180-day appeal window, peer-to-peer option, and free access to claim file and clinical criteria
